_doc: Hand-crafted minimal neutral-stage end-state for the first ion-driver
cross-reference between Python and legacy MATLAB.
Layout: 1 molecule -> 2 atoms in the standard 2N convention
(atom 1 of molecule i at index i; atom 2 at index num_molecules+i).
With N=1 that means atom 1 at row 0, atom 2 at row 1.
All values are plain IEEE 754 doubles, parsed identically by
Python json.load and MATLAB jsondecode. Positions in Angstrom,
velocities in Angstrom/ps, mass in atomic mass units, time in ps.
Choices:
- non-zero z and vz on each atom -> exposes the legacy 2D-radial
E_pot bug and the legacy missing-vz E_kin bug.
- asymmetric per-atom velocities -> any per-atom bookkeeping bug
shows up immediately.
- droplet_radius = 4.0 A with atoms at r ~ 3.6-3.7 A so the atoms
sit near the erf transition and the 3D-vs-2D radial difference
is numerically visible.
- pair separation ~7.48 A with q=q=+1 -> partner Coulomb energy
~1.92 eV per pair, of which the legacy t=0 bookkeeping drops half.
All stochastic ion physics is disabled in both scripts (no
collisions, no mass attachment, no v-dependent sigma). No
propagation step is taken on either side.
